<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#Item14_PrincipalAccountingFeesAnd_170146">PRINCIPAL   ACCOUNTING FEES AND SERVICES</a></font></p>    </td>
<td width="5%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:5.6%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">106</font></p>    </td>   </tr>
<tr>
<td width="10%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.44%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="83%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:83.96%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="5%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:5.6%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>
<tr>
<td width="10%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.44%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><u><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;"><a href="#Partiv_170227" title="Click to goto ">PART&nbsp;IV</a></font></u></b></p>    </td>
<td width="83%" valign="top" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:83.96%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="5%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:5.6%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">PART&nbsp;I</font></b><a name="Parti_162319"></a></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">This Annual Report filed on Form&nbsp;10-K and the information incorporated herein by reference includes statements that are, or may be deemed, &#147;forward-looking statements.&#148;&#160; In some cases, these forward-looking statements can be identified by the use of forward-looking terminology, including the terms &#147;believes,&#148; &#147;estimates,&#148; &#147;anticipates,&#148; &#147;expects,&#148; &#147;plans,&#148; &#147;intends,&#148; &#147;may,&#148; &#147;could,&#148; &#147;might,&#148; &#147;will,&#148; &#147;should,&#148; &#147;approximately&#148; or, in each case, their negative or other variations thereon or comparable terminology, although not all forward-looking statements contain these words.&#160; They appear in a number of places throughout this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K and include statements regarding our intentions, beliefs, projections, outlook, analyses or current expectations concerning, among other things, our ongoing and planned preclinical studies and clinical trials, the timing of and our ability to make regulatory filings and obtain and maintain regulatory approvals for our product candidates, the degree of clinical utility of our products, particularly in specific patient populations, expectations regarding clinical trial data, our results of operations, financial condition, liquidity, prospects, growth and strategies, the industry in which we operate and the trends that may affect the industry or us.</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">By their nature, forward-looking statements involve risks and uncertainties because they relate to events, competitive dynamics, and industry change and depend on the economic circumstances that may or may not occur in the future or may occur on longer or shorter timelines than anticipated.&#160; We caution you that forward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance and that our actual results of operations, financial condition and liquidity, and events in the industry in which we operate may differ materially from the forward-looking statements contained herein.</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Any forward-looking statements that we make in this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K speak only as of the date of this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K, and we undertake no obligation to update such statements to reflect events or circumstances after the date of this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K or to reflect the occurrence of unanticipated events.</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">You should also read carefully the factors described in the &#147;Risk Factors&#148; section of this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K to better understand the risks and uncertainties inherent in our business and underlying any forward-looking statements.&#160; You are advised, however, to consult any further disclosures we make on related subjects in our Quarterly Reports on Form&nbsp;10-Q, Current Reports on Form&nbsp;8-K and our website.</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">T</font></i><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">he TESARO logo is a trademark of TESARO,&nbsp;Inc. in the United States and in other selected countries.&#160; All other brand names or trademarks appearing in this report are the property of their respective holders.&#160; Unless the context requires otherwise, references in this report to &#147;TESARO&#148;, the &#147;Company,&#148; &#147;we,&#148; &#147;us,&#148; and &#147;our&#148; refer to TESARO,&nbsp;Inc.</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 1. BUSINESS</font></b><a name="Item1_Business_162333"></a></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Overview</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are an oncology-focused biopharmaceutical company dedicated to improving the lives of cancer patients.&#160; We acquire, in-license and develop oncology product candidates and, if approved for marketing, we intend to commercialize these products globally.&#160; We have in-licensed and are currently developing three product candidates, rolapitant, niraparib and TSR-011, and in March&nbsp;2014 we initiated our immuno-oncology platform strategy by entering into a collaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio,&nbsp;Inc., or AnaptysBio, for the discovery and development of antibodies for specified immuno-oncology targets.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Rolapitant</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> is a potent and long-acting neurokinin-1, or NK-1, receptor antagonist currently in Phase&nbsp;3 (oral formulation) and Phase 1 (intravenous, or IV, formulation) clinical trials for the prevention of chemotherapy induced nausea and vomiting, or CINV.&#160; In December&nbsp;2013, we announced top-line results for two Phase 3 trials of rolapitant.&#160; The primary endpoint was successfully achieved in each trial, and preparations in support of our anticipated submission to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, or FDA, in mid-2014 of a new drug application, or NDA, for oral rolapitant are ongoing.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Niraparib,</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> formerly known as MK-4827, is an orally active and potent poly (ADP-ribose) polymerase, or PARP, inhibitor.&#160; In July&nbsp;2013, we dosed the first patient in a Phase 3 clinical trial evaluating niraparib for the treatment of patients with high grade serous, </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">platinum sensitive, relapsed ovarian cancer.&#160; We expect to dose the first patient in a Phase 3 clinical trial evaluating niraparib in breast cancer patients with germline BRCA mutations in the first quarter of 2014.&#160; We also are collaborating with the Sarcoma Alliance for Research through Collaboration, or SARC, to evaluate niraparib in combination with temozolomide for the treatment of Ewing&#146;s sarcoma.&#160; We may also evaluate niraparib for the treatment of gastric, lung, and prostate cancer.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4<a name="PB_4_162410_7056"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='4',FILE='C:\JMS\skris\13-25458-1\task6623766\25458-1-bi.htm',USER='105340',CD='Mar 14 04:52 2014' -->

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">TSR-011</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> is an orally available targeted anti-cancer agent which is a potent inhibitor of both anaplastic lymphoma kinase, or ALK, </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">and tropomyosin-related kinase, or TRK, </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">currently in a Phase&nbsp;1/2a dose escalation clinical trial in cancer patients.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have identified the maximum tolerated dose of TSR-011 and are now evaluating fractionated 60 milligram (mg) doses of TSR-011 in patients with ALK or TRK expression, including those with ALK-positive, or ALK+, and TRK-positive, or TRK+, non-small cell lung cancer, or NSCLC, who have not been previously treated with ALK inhibitors, those with ALK+ NSCLC who have progressed during treatment with other ALK inhibitors, and in those patients with other tumor types driven by ALK or TRK.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2014, we entered into a c</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ollaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio,&nbsp;Inc., or AnaptysBio, a privately-held therapeutic antibody company.&#160; Under the terms of this agreement, we obtained an exclusive, royalty-bearing, sublicenseable worldwide license to research, develop, manufacture, market and sell products incorporating </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">certain specified immunotherapy antibodies</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> developed using AnaptysBio&#146;s </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">proprietary technology for the discovery, generation and optimization of antibodies.&#160; Specifically, we received exclusive rights to monospecific antibody product candidates targeting TIM-3, LAG-3 and PD-1 and dual-reactive antibody product candidates targeting PD-1/TIM-3 and PD-1/LAG-3.&#160; Under the agreement, AnaptysBio is responsible for performing initial discovery and development of therapeutic antibodies against immune checkpoint proteins, with the goal of generating immunotherapy antibodies for use in the treatment of cancer.&#160; We are responsible for all subsequent preclinical, clinical, regulatory, manufacturing and other activities necessary to develop and commercialize antibodies selected under each of three development programs, and we are obligated to use commercially reasonable efforts to research, develop or commercialize at least one product under each development program.&#160; PD-1, or programmed cell death protein 1, is a key immune checkpoint molecule that can limit T-cell-mediated immune responses. The presence of the PD-1 ligand, or PD-L1, has been identified on many tumor types, and expression of PD-L1 has been linked to poor clinical outcomes in a variety of cancers.&#160; Anti-PD-1 antibodies have demonstrated in vivo efficacy in tumor models and have shown promising results in several clinical studies.&#160; We anticipate beginning clinical trials using TSR-042, the lead anti-PD-1 antibody that we have in-licensed as part of the agreement with AnaptysBio, in mid-2015.&#160; In addition, we plan to evaluate our immuno-oncology anti-tumor agents, such as TSR-042, in combination preclinical pharmacology studies with TSR-011, niraparib and other anti-tumor agents.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition to potential candidates arising from our agreement with AnaptysBio, we intend to continue to leverage the experience and competencies of our senior management team to identify, acquire, develop and commercialize cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care products that are potentially safer and more effective than existing treatments.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2010, we in-licensed from OPKO Health,&nbsp;Inc., or OPKO, the exclusive worldwide rights to our first product candidate, rolapitant, a long-acting NK-1 receptor antagonist that is being developed for the prevention of CINV.&#160; OPKO had acquired certain NK-1 receptor related assets, including rolapitant, in 2010 from Schering-Plough Corporation, or Schering-Plough, as part of a United States Federal Trade Commission, or FTC, requirement to divest certain assets in connection with Schering-Plough&#146;s combination with Merck&nbsp;&amp; Co.,&nbsp;Inc., or Merck.&#160; Prior to its divestiture of rolapitant, Schering-Plough evaluated rolapitant in over 1,000 subjects, including studies for the prevention of post-operative nausea and vomiting, or PONV, and chronic cough, and completed a Phase&nbsp;2 clinical trial in patients at high risk for CINV.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2011, we in-licensed the exclusive worldwide rights to TSR-011 from Amgen,&nbsp;Inc., or Amgen.&#160; TSR-011 is a small molecule inhibitor of ALK and TRK for the treatment of NSCLC and potentially other cancer indications.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In May&nbsp;2012, we entered into a license agreement with Merck Sharp&nbsp;&amp; Dohme Corp., a subsidiary of Merck, under which we obtained exclusive, worldwide rights to certain patents and non-exclusive rights to certain Merck know-how, to research, develop, manufacture, market and sell niraparib for all therapeutic and prophylactic uses in humans.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Upon successful development and regulatory approval of any of our product candidates, we intend to make them available to cancer patients in North America, Europe and China through our own commercialization efforts.&#160; At this time, we believe that if we commercialize our products directly in these geographic areas, we will receive a greater return on our investment than if we license these products to third parties for sale.&#160; We believe this because our management team has experience commercializing products in these geographic areas and has an understanding of the relevant sales, marketing and reimbursement frameworks unique to these areas.&#160; In addition to developing internal commercial capabilities within these three geographic areas, we intend to establish a network of licensees and distributors for our products in other geographic areas.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We were founded in March&nbsp;2010 by former executives of MGI PHARMA, which was acquired by Eisai&nbsp;Co.,&nbsp;Ltd. in 2008.&#160; While at MGI PHARMA, our senior management team collaborated in the clinical development and commercialization of several cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care drugs, including Aloxi (palonosetron HCl).&#160; In marketing rolapitant, we believe we will be able to leverage our senior management team&#146;s long-standing experience with key opinion leaders, patient groups, payors, oncology networks, cancer centers, oncologists, oncology nurses, and pharmacists.&#160; Given this experience, we believe we can successfully develop and, if approved, commercialize rolapitant, and grow the market for NK-1 receptor antagonists.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our senior management team includes our Chief Executive Officer and co-founder, Leon (Lonnie) Moulder,&nbsp;Jr., and our President and Chief Scientific Officer and co-founder, Mary Lynne Hedley, Ph.D.&#160; Mr.&nbsp;Moulder has been involved in a number of biopharmaceutical companies, including as vice-chairman of the board of directors and president and chief executive officer of Abraxis BioScience,&nbsp;Inc., vice chairman of Eisai Corporation of North America and president and chief executive officer of MGI PHARMA.&#160; Dr.&nbsp;Hedley has served as the executive vice president and chief scientific officer of Abraxis BioScience,&nbsp;Inc., executive vice president of Eisai Corporation of North America and executive vice president and chief scientific officer of MGI PHARMA.&#160; Dr.&nbsp;Hedley also co-founded and served as chief executive officer and president of ZYCOS,&nbsp;Inc., a biotechnology company which was acquired by MGI PHARMA.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5<a name="PB_5_162441_5335"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On June&nbsp;28, 2012, we completed our initial public offering through which we sold 6,000,000 shares of common stock at a price of $13.50 per share.&#160; The shares began trading on the NASDAQ Global Select Market on June&nbsp;29, 2012, and the transaction closed on July&nbsp;3, 2012.&#160; Immediately prior to the closing of the offering, all outstanding shares of our convertible preferred stock converted into 19,410,490 shares of common stock.&#160; On July&nbsp;23, 2012, the underwriters of our initial public offering purchased an additional 430,183 shares by exercising a portion of the over-allotment option granted to them in connection with the initial public offering.&#160; As a result of the closing of the initial public offering and subsequent exercise of the over-allotment option, we received aggregate net proceeds of approximately $78.0 million, which is net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses.&#160; In March&nbsp;2013, we sold </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5,428,000 shares of common stock in an underwritten public offering pursuant to a registration statement on Form&nbsp;S-1, at a price of $18.00 per share, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $91.3 million</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">, which is net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In February&nbsp;2014, we sold </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3,200,000 shares of common stock in an underwritten public offering pursuant to a registration statement on Form&nbsp;S-3, at a price of $31.50 per share, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $94.1 million</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">, which is net of underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.&#160; The proceeds of our February&nbsp;2014 offering are not reflected in the financial results reported in this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our common stock trades on the NASDAQ Global Select Market, or NASDAQ, under the trading symbol &#147;TSRO.&#148;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Our Strategy</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our strategy is to leverage the experience and competencies of our senior management team to identify, acquire and develop promising drug candidates and to commercialize cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care products that are potentially safer and more effective than existing treatments.&#160; This strategy is based upon our belief that it is efficient and effective to focus our efforts on cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care because the same treatment centers and healthcare professionals can be covered by a single sales and marketing organization.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The key components of our strategy are:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Rapidly Develop and Successfully Commercialize Rolapitant for the Prevention of CINV.</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; In early&nbsp;2012 we enrolled the first patient in our global Phase&nbsp;3 clinical program for rolapitant, approximately one year after in-licensing this product candidate.&#160; We reported top-line results for two of our three Phase 3 clinical trials of oral rolapitant in December&nbsp;2013.&#160; We expect to report top-line results for the third Phase 3 clinical trial in early 2014.&#160; Our ongoing Phase&nbsp;3 clinical program for oral rolapitant includes investigative sites in over 25 countries.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In January&nbsp;2014, in a single ascending dose study we identified a dose of our IV formulation of rolapitant that we believe is likely to achieve bioequivalence to a 200mg dose of oral rolapitant, and we may compare the safety and exposure of IV rolapitant to the oral formulation in a subsequent clinical trial, which would commence in the second half of 2014.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We believe that we are well positioned to maximize the commercial potential of rolapitant.&#160; At MGI PHARMA, in 2003 our senior management team successfully launched and commercialized Aloxi, a 5-HT3 receptor antagonist for the prevention of CINV, in the United States.&#160; Aloxi, based on revenues, became the largest product in its class in 2006, despite being the fourth 5-HT3 receptor antagonist to market in the United States and competing with products sold by GlaxoSmithKline&nbsp;plc, Roche Holding&nbsp;Ltd. and Sanofi&nbsp;S.A.&#160; We intend to leverage the experience that our senior management team gained at MGI PHARMA to establish rolapitant, if approved, as part of the standard of care for the prevention of CINV in patients who, per established treatment guidelines, could benefit from an NK-1 receptor antagonist, in addition to treatment with a 5-HT3 receptor antagonist.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Continue the Clinical Development of and Successfully Commercialize Niraparib for the Treatment of Cancers that are Susceptible to PARP Inhibition.</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We are evaluating niraparib for the treatment of patients with high grade serous, </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">platinum sensitive, relapsed ovarian cancer in a Phase 3 clinical study, which we commenced in July&nbsp;2013.&#160; We also plan to evaluate niraparib in breast cancer patients with germline BRCA mutations in a Phase 3 clinical trial, in which we expect to dose the first patient in the first quarter of 2014.&#160; We also are collaborating with SARC to evaluate niraparib in combination with temozolomide for the treatment of Ewing&#146;s sarcoma.&#160; We may also evaluate niraparib for the treatment of gastric, lung, and prostate cancer, and we plan to evaluate niraparib in combination preclinical pharmacology studies with our immuno-oncology anti-tumor agents.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Advance TSR-011 Through Clinical Trials for the Treatment of NSCLC and Other Tumor Types Associated with ALK and TRK Mutations.</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We are pursuing a development pathway that we believe, if successful, will enable us to reduce the time to receive regulatory approval for this product candidate.&#160; In September&nbsp;2012, we</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">6<a name="PB_6_162548_5796"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">filed an investigational new drug application, or IND, for TSR-011 with the FDA that became effective in October&nbsp;2012, and in November&nbsp;2012, we announced that we had dosed the first patient in a Phase&nbsp;1/2a dose escalation clinical trial of TSR-011 in cancer patients.&#160; We have identified the maximum tolerated dose of TSR-011 and are now evaluating fractionated 60mg doses of TSR-011 in patients with ALK or TRK expression, including those with ALK+ and TRK+ NSCLC who have not been previously treated with ALK inhibitors, those with ALK+ NSCLC who have progressed during treatment with other ALK inhibitors, and those patients with other tumor types driven by ALK or TRK.&#160; ALK is a key driver of multiple types of cancers, including subsets of NSCLC, neuroblastoma and lymphoma.&#160; TRK may drive tumor growth in patients with TRK+ NSCLC, colorectal and thyroid cancer.&#160; In order to maximize the commercial potential of TSR-011, we plan to study TSR-011 in multiple tumor types and treatment settings.&#160; We believe that TSR-011 may be differentiated from crizotinib, currently the only marketed ALK inhibitor, as well as other ALK inhibitors in development, due to its potency, specificity and activity on specific mutant ALK proteins and by its pharmacological properties, which could attract clinical investigators and patients to our clinical trials.&#160; We </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">also plan to evaluate TSR-011 in combination preclinical pharmacology studies with our immuno-oncology anti-tumor agents.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Identify and Advance Potential Antibody Product Candidates Under Our Collaboration and Exclusive License Agreement with AnaptysBio.</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; In March&nbsp;2014, we entered into a c</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ollaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio for the </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">discovery and development of immunotherapy antibodies against certain validated immuno-oncology targets.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Under the agreement, we received exclusive rights to products based on AnaptysBio&#146;s </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">proprietary technology for the discovery, generation and optimization of antibodies targeting certain immune checkpoint proteins.&#160; Specifically, we received exclusive rights to monospecific antibody product candidates targeting TIM-3, LAG-3 and PD-1 and dual</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">-</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">reactive antibody product candidates targeting PD-1/TIM-3 and PD-1/LAG-3.&#160; We have selected a lead monospecific antibody for the PD-1 program, TSR-042, and we intend to select other lead candidates and carry out preclinical, clinical, regulatory and other activities necessary to develop and commercialize antibodies selected under each of the three development programs.&#160; We believe that these therapeutic antibodies will form the basis of a strategic platform that will potentially enable us to develop novel monotherapy and combination-based approaches with immuno-oncology and other anti-cancer agents in a variety of indications.&#160; Specifically, we believe this platform will enable us to initiate clinical development in new tumor indications, not addressed with our current product candidates, and to study combination approaches in the clinic, both with our existing product candidates and potentially with new candidates we either in-license or access through collaborative transactions with others.&#160; We expect that TSR-042 will begin clinical trials in mid-2015.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><b><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">In-license or Acquire Additional Product Candidates to Create a Balanced Product Portfolio.</font></i></b><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We intend to in-license or acquire additional product candidates across various stages of development.&#160; We do not have, nor do we intend to build, drug discovery capabilities.&#160; We intend to focus on product candidates that we believe are differentiated from existing cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care products and that have well-defined, and potentially expeditious, clinical and regulatory pathways.&#160; Our criteria for selecting therapeutic product candidates for acquisition includes consideration of potential diagnostics or specific clinical criteria that we believe would allow us to enrich our clinical study population for cancer patients who are more likely to respond to the compound.&#160; We believe that our three current product candidates, and potential product candidates under our collaboration with </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">AnaptysBio,</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> have these characteristics.&#160; We believe that our ability to execute on this strategy is due to our senior management team&#146;s previous experience with in-licensing and acquiring cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care products on mutually advantageous terms, and their prior success in developing and obtaining regulatory approval for these compounds, and developing markets for and commercializing these products.&#160; Our objective is to build a portfolio of cancer therapeutics and oncology supportive care products that is balanced by stage of development, resource requirements and development risk.&#160; We categorize acquisition or in-licensing targets as follows:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Lower risk, later-stage assets that serve as a foundation for building a commercial business.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We continue to seek, and may in-license or acquire, late-stage product candidates, such as rolapitant, with well-defined regulatory and clinical development paths.&#160; By doing so, we believe that we can minimize to some degree the risks of development and regulatory approval.&#160; Having multiple products at, or near, a commercial stage will allow us to utilize the sales and marketing and medical affairs organizations we intend to build in a cost-effective manner.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Mid-stage assets supported by early clinical study results indicating activity and adequate safety.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We continue to seek, and may in-license or acquire, mid-stage product candidates and seek to advance them to final clinical testing, regulatory approval and commercialization.&#160; In identifying mid-stage assets, we intend to focus on assets that we believe demonstrate activity and adequate safety based on early clinical testing (i.e.,&nbsp;Phase&nbsp;1 or 2 clinical trials).&#160; Assets at this stage generally have more risk of eventual success than later-stage assets.&#160; We believe that when we acquired rights to niraparib in May&nbsp;2012, it was representative of this type of asset.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 1.25in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Early-stage, potentially transformational assets associated with signals of effectiveness or patient selection approaches, by use of tools such as biomarkers.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We continue to seek, and may in-license or acquire, early-stage assets that we can develop from preclinical status to commercialized products.&#160; For this category of assets, we intend to focus on those compounds for which signals of effectiveness are demonstrated during <i>in vitro</i> or <i>in vivo</i> preclinical testing.&#160; Ideally, the early-stage assets we in-license or acquire will exhibit signals of effectiveness for identifiable subpopulations of cancer patients, thereby allowing for the selection of cancer patients during clinical testing who are most likely to respond to treatment.&#160; We believe this will lead to more efficient and effective clinical trials and, if approved, better prescription patterns, providing for the best potential patient outcomes.&#160; We believe that this more personalized medicine approach to cancer therapy will allow for a more rapid and</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">7<a name="PB_7_162743_2897"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
