<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Because of the breadth of these various fraud and abuse laws, it is possible that some of our business activities could be subject to challenge under one or more of such laws.&#160; Such a challenge could have material adverse effects on our business, financial condition and results of operations.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Patents and Proprietary Rights</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have in-licensed four patent portfolios, one each for our rolapitant, niraparib, ALK/TRK and immunotherapeutic antibodies programs.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our NK-1 receptor antagonist portfolio, which relates to rolapitant, consists of eight patent families currently being prosecuted or maintained, which include applications and patents directed to compositions of matter, formulations (including oral and IV), solid forms, methods of treatment (including both delayed and acute onset nausea and/or vomiting and timing of administration in relation to chemotherapy) and methods of preparing rolapitant.&#160; Rolapitant is a NK-1 receptor antagonist being developed for the prevention of chemotherapy induced nausea and/or vomiting.&#160; The portfolio licensed for rolapitant consists of twelve issued United States patents and 112 issued non-United States patents across the eight families.&#160; In the patent family covering the composition of matter, we have three issued United States patents and 67 issued non-United States patents.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our PARP inhibitor portfolio includes three patent families relating to niraparib and two other patent families relating to MK-2512, the backup PARP inhibitor compound licensed from Merck that is not currently being developed.&#160; All five of the patent families are being prosecuted or maintained by Merck in consultation with us.&#160; The three patent families relating to niraparib include applications and patents directed to compositions of matter, methods of treatment (including treatment of cancer and other diseases), and particular salts of niraparib.&#160; Of these three patent families, the first claims a broad genus of compounds that encompasses niraparib and uses thereof.&#160; This first family consists of applications pending in Europe, Canada and India and patents issued in Australia, Japan and China.&#160; The second family, which claims niraparib, presently comprises 64 issued patents worldwide, including a patent in the United States as well as patents in several European countries.&#160; This second family also has applications pending worldwide.&#160; The third patent family relating to niraparib is directed to particular salts of niraparib.&#160; This third family is being prosecuted worldwide, and patents have been issued in the United States, Russia, New Zealand and South Africa.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our anaplastic lymphoma kinase portfolio consists of three patent families directed to both compositions of matter and methods of treating certain cancer sub-populations whose tumors express mutant ALK protein.&#160; These three patent families are at early stages of prosecution.&#160; No patents are currently issued, but we have submitted two patent applications in the United States and 19 patent applications outside of the United States.&#160; The third family consists of a Patent Cooperation Treaty application which is published and a Taiwanese application.&#160; The national phase deadline for conversion of the pending PCT application expires in June&nbsp;2014.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our immunotherapeutic antibodies portfolio presently includes a United States patent application covering particular lead antibodies to one identified target of interest.&#160; Additional filings are contemplated with respect to other targets, and it is expected that worldwide filings will be pursued.&#160; We have rights to all patents owned or controlled by our collaborator, AnaptysBio, to the extent that they claim the manufacture, composition, or use of an antagonist antibody developed under the program.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Intellectual Property Protection Strategy</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We currently seek, and intend to continue seeking patent protection whenever available for any patentable aspects of our existing products or product candidates and related technology or any new products or product candidates we acquire in the future.&#160; Where our intellectual property is not protectable by patents, we seek to protect this through other means, including maintenance of trade secrets and careful protection of our proprietary information.&#160; Our license from Merck for niraparib requires Merck to, subject to certain exceptions, prosecute and maintain, upon consultation with us, its patent rights as they relate to the licensed compounds.&#160; If Merck decides to cease prosecution of the licensed patent rights, we have the right to take over such prosecution activities.&#160; Our licenses from OPKO for rolapitant and from Amgen for TSR-011 grant us the right to control all prosecution and maintenance activities for the licensed compounds, at our sole discretion.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The patent positions of biopharmaceutical companies like us are generally uncertain and involve complex legal, scientific and factual questions.&#160; In addition, the coverage claimed in a patent application can be significantly reduced before the patent is issued, and its scope can be reinterpreted after issuance.&#160; Consequently, we do not know whether any of the product candidates we in-license or acquire will be protectable or remain protected by enforceable patents.&#160; We cannot predict whether the patent applications we are currently pursuing will issue as patents in any particular jurisdiction, and furthermore, we cannot determine whether the claims of any issued patents will provide sufficient proprietary protection to protect us from competitors, or will be challenged, circumvented or invalidated by third parties.&#160; Because patent applications in the United States and certain other jurisdictions are maintained in secrecy for 18&nbsp;months, and since publication of</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">33<a name="PB_33_103646_536"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='33',FILE='C:\JMS\C901710\13-25458-1\task6624594\25458-1-bm.htm',USER='C901710',CD='Mar 14 11:21 2014' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">discoveries in the scientific or patent literature often lags behind actual discoveries, we cannot be certain of the priority of inventions covered by pending patent applications.&#160; In March&nbsp;2013, the United States transitioned to a &#145;first to file&#146; system in which the first inventor to file&nbsp;a patent application will be entitled to the patent.&#160; Previously, in the United States, the first to make the claimed invention was entitled to the patent.&#160; Moreover, we may have to participate in interference proceedings declared by the PTO or a foreign patent office to determine priority of invention and/or in post-grant challenge proceedings (such as oppositions) that challenge priority of invention or other features of patentability.&#160; Such proceedings could result in substantial cost, even if the eventual outcome is favorable to us.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Although we currently have issued patents covering a number of different attributes of our products, and pending applications on others, there can be no assurance that any issued patents would be held valid by a court of competent jurisdiction.&#160; An adverse outcome could subject us to significant liabilities to third parties, require disputed rights to be licensed from third parties or require us to cease using specific compounds or technology.&#160; To the extent prudent, we intend to bring litigation against third parties that we believe are infringing our patents.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The term of individual patents depends upon the legal term of the patents in the countries in which they are obtained.&#160; In most countries in which we file, the patent term is 20&nbsp;years from the earliest date of filing a non-provisional patent application.&#160; In the United States, a patent&#146;s term may be lengthened by patent term adjustment, which compensates a patentee for administrative delays by the PTO in granting a patent, or may be shortened if a patent is terminally disclaimed over another patent.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In the United States, the term of a patent that covers an FDA-approved drug or biological product may also be eligible for patent term extension, which permits patent term restoration as compensation for the patent term lost during the FDA regulatory review process.&#160; The Hatch-Waxman Act permits a patent term extension of up to five years beyond the expiration of the patent.&#160; The length of the patent term extension is related to the length of time the drug or biologic is under regulatory review.&#160; Patent extension cannot extend the remaining term of a patent beyond a total of 14&nbsp;years from the date of product approval and only one patent applicable to an approved drug may be extended.&#160; Patent term extension is available only if the approval of the product represents the first permitted commercial marketing of the active ingredient.&#160; Similar provisions are available in Europe and other non-U.S. jurisdictions to extend the term of a patent that covers an approved drug.&#160; In the future, if and when our products receive FDA approval, we expect to apply for patent term extensions on patents covering those products.&#160; We intend to seek patent term adjustments and extensions to any of our issued patents in any jurisdiction where these are available; however, there is no guarantee that the applicable authorities, including the FDA in the United States, will agree with our assessment of whether such extensions should be granted, and even if granted, the length of such adjustments or extensions.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To protect our rights to any of our issued patents and proprietary information, we may need to litigate against infringing third parties, or avail ourselves of the courts or participate in hearings to determine the scope and validity of those patents or other proprietary rights.&#160; These types of proceedings are often costly and could be very time-consuming to us, and we cannot be certain that the deciding authorities will rule&nbsp;in our favor.&#160; An unfavorable decision could result in the invalidation or a limitation in the scope of our patents or forfeiture of the rights associated with our patents or pending patent applications.&#160; Any such decision could result in our key technologies not being protectable, allowing third parties to use our technology without being required to pay us licensing fees, or may compel us to license needed technologies from third parties to avoid infringing third-party patent and proprietary rights.&#160; Such a decision could even result in the invalidation or a limitation in the scope of our patents or could cause us to lose our rights under existing issued patents or not to have rights granted under our pending patent applications.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition, we intend to seek orphan drug status in jurisdictions in which it is available.&#160; An orphan drug designation may be granted where a drug or biological product is developed specifically to treat a rare or uncommon medical condition.&#160; If a product which has an orphan drug designation subsequently receives the first regulatory approval for the indication for which it has such designation, the product is entitled to orphan exclusivity, meaning that the applicable regulatory authority may not approve any other applications to market the same drug or biologic for the same indication, except in certain very limited circumstances, for a period of seven years in the United States and ten years in the European Union.&#160; Orphan drug designation does not prevent competitors from developing or marketing different drugs or biologics for an indication, or the same drug or biologic for different indications.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We also rely on trade secret protection for our confidential and proprietary information.&#160; Although we take steps to protect our proprietary information and trade secrets, including through contractual means with our employees and</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">34<a name="PB_34_121734_5335"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">consultants, no assurance can be given that others will not independently develop substantially equivalent proprietary information and techniques or otherwise gain access to our trade secrets or disclose such technology, or that we can meaningfully protect our trade secrets.&#160; It is our policy to require our employees, consultants, outside scientific collaborators, sponsored researchers and other advisors to execute confidentiality agreements upon the commencement of employment or consulting relationships with us.&#160; These agreements provide that all confidential information developed or made known to the individual during the course of the individual&#146;s relationship with us is to be kept confidential and not disclosed to third parties except in specific circumstances.&#160; In the case of employees, the agreements provide that all inventions conceived by the individual shall be our exclusive property.&#160; There can be no assurance, however, that these agreements will provide meaningful protection or adequate remedies for our trade secrets in the event of unauthorized use or disclosure of such information.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">NK-1 Receptor Antagonists</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have an exclusive, worldwide license from OPKO to a portfolio of patents related to rolapitant, including issued claims covering the composition of matter and certain formulations and methods of use.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">United States Patent 7,049,320 claims composition of matter for the chemical composition of rolapitant, and a sister patent claims compositions of matter of related compounds.&#160; Corresponding applications and issued patents in multiple foreign jurisdictions have similar composition of matter claims.&#160; This family of patents and/or applications has a patent term of at least until December&nbsp;2022.&#160; With the patent term adjustment, United States Patent 7,049,320 expires in December&nbsp;2023.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Many jurisdictions also grant extensions of patent term, typically up to five years, for post-issuance regulatory delay.&#160; Only one patent may be extended per approved product.&#160; We believe that patent term extension under the Hatch-Waxman Act could be available to extend our patent exclusivity for rolapitant by up to five years in the United States depending on timing of our first approval.&#160; In Europe, we believe that patent term extension under supplementary protection certificate could also be available for an additional five years depending on timing of our first approval.&#160; There is no guarantee that the maximum allowable extension will be granted, and any extension granted may be shorter than this, or not granted at all.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">United States Patent 7,563,801 claims oral pharmaceutical formulations of rolapitant, including capsule formulations.&#160; A sister patent, United States Patent 7,981,905, claims methods of treating nausea and/or emesis by administration of pharmaceutical formulations of rolapitant.&#160; Corresponding patents and applications in multiple foreign jurisdictions similarly have claims directed to pharmaceutical formulations of rolapitant and uses thereof.&#160; This family of patents and/or applications has a patent term of at least until April&nbsp;2027.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">United States Patent 8,178,550 claims the hydrochloride monohydrate polymorphic form of the chemical composition of rolapitant.&#160; Corresponding applications and issued patents in multiple foreign jurisdictions have similar claims to various polymorphic forms of rolapitant.&#160; This family of patents and/or applications has a patent term of at least until April&nbsp;2027.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">A patent application directed to a tablet formulation of rolapitant is allowed in the United States.&#160; Corresponding patent applications and issued patents in multiple other jurisdictions.&#160; This family of patents and/or applications has a patent term of at least until March&nbsp;2028.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Patent applications directed towards IV formulations of rolapitant (including in the form of a micelle formulation) are pending in the United States and multiple foreign jurisdictions.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">PARP Inhibitor</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have an exclusive, worldwide license from Merck to a portfolio of patents related to two inhibitors of poly (ADP-ribose) polymerase: niraparib and MK-2512.&#160; The three patent families that relate to niraparib include one issued United States patent: United States Patent 8,071,623.&#160; This patent has a term of until March&nbsp;2030.&#160; We have filed corresponding applications and have been issued 64 corresponding patents in multiple other jurisdictions worldwide.&#160; Unless their patent terms are extended due to delays by the responsible patent office or regulatory authority, or are shortened by terminal disclaimers, the patents in this family (other than United States Patent 8,071,623) will expire in approximately January&nbsp;2028.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">35<a name="PB_35_121734_5796"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='35',FILE='C:\JMS\C901810\13-25458-1\task6623673\25458-1-bo.htm',USER='C901810',CD='Mar 14 04:36 2014' -->

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The patent family corresponding to United States Application No.&nbsp;13/091,427 discloses and claims a broad genus of compounds that encompasses niraparib.&#160; This family includes applications pending in multiple jurisdictions and one patent issued in Japan.&#160; Unless their patent terms are extended due to delays by the responsible patent office or regulatory authority, or shortened by terminal disclaimers, the patents in this family will expire approximately April&nbsp;2027.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The third patent family relating to niraparib discloses and claims particular salts of niraparib.&#160; This family includes applications pending in multiple jurisdictions and patents issued in the United States, New Zealand and South Africa.&#160; Unless their patent terms are extended due to delays by the responsible patent office or regulatory authority, or shortened by terminal disclaimers, the patents in this family will expire approximately January&nbsp;2029.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We believe that patent term extension under the Hatch-Waxman Act could be available to extend our patent exclusivity for niraparib by up to five years in the United States, depending on timing of our first approval.&#160; Such an extension would be available, if at all, on only one United States patent.&#160; With respect to Europe, we believe that supplementary protection certificates (which are issued on a country-by-country basis in Europe) could add up to five years to the patent term of a patent issued in each European country, depending on timing of our first approval.&#160; There is no guarantee that any extension will be granted, and even if granted, the extension may be less than the maximum allowable extension.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK) Inhibitor</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have an exclusive, worldwide license from Amgen to a portfolio of patents related to inhibitors of anaplastic lymphoma kinase, including TSR-011.&#160; Our ALK portfolio consists of three patent families.&#160; The first patent family, corresponding to International Patent Application number PCT/US2011/035186 and directed toward novel compositions of matter and methods of treating certain cancer sub-populations whose tumors express an ALK fusion protein, is pending in multiple jurisdictions (including the United States).&#160; The second family, corresponding to International Patent Application number PCT/US2011/045703 and directed toward the genus of compounds that includes TSR-011 is pending in multiple jurisdictions.&#160; These applications, if issued, would expire in 2031.&#160; The third family, directed to methods of treating ALK resistance, is not yet published.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Manufacturing</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2012, we entered into a process development and manufacturing services agreement with Hovione Inter Limited, or Hovione, under which Hovione will provide certain process development and manufacturing services in connection with the manufacture of rolapitant.&#160; The agreement also provides that if Hovione is successful in implementing the manufacturing process and the agreement is not terminated by us, Hovione would also manufacture certain commercial quantities of rolapitant.&#160; Under the agreement, we will pay Hovione for services in accordance with the terms of work plans, which we will enter into from time to time.&#160; Each party to the agreement is subject to customary indemnification provisions.&#160; Unless terminated earlier, the agreement will continue until the later of the fifth anniversary of (i)&nbsp;all development services under the last work plan executed in accordance with the terms of the agreement or (ii)&nbsp;the first launch date of the product to occur in any of the following jurisdictions: Europe; Japan; or the United States.&#160; The agreement may be extended by agreement of the parties.&#160; We may terminate the agreement at the end of each phase of the initial work plan and may terminate any work plan executed after the initial work plan upon at least thirty (30)&nbsp;days&#146; prior written notice to Hovione.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Additionally, we currently contract with other third parties for the manufacture of our product candidates for preclinical studies and clinical trials and intend to do so in the future.&#160; We currently work with one contract manufacturer, or CMO, Hovione, for the production of rolapitant drug substance, one other CMO for the production of a rolapitant oral drug product for Phase&nbsp;3 clinical trials, and one other CMO for the production of a rolapitant IV drug product for clinical use.&#160; We utilized CMOs for the manufacture of TSR-011 for use in preclinical and Phase 1/2a clinical trials.&#160; To meet our needs with respect to further clinical development, we have contracted with additional CMOs for the manufacture of clinical supplies.&#160; Existing inventory for niraparib drug substance and drug product from Merck has provided the initial clinical trial material needed for our niraparib clinical program.&#160; We have agreements in place with multiple CMOs for the further production of niraparib (both drug substance and drug product) to meet our ongoing clinical supply needs.&#160; We plan to select one or more CMOs capable of producing biologics for the manufacture of TSR-042 and other antibody products.&#160; For each of our product candidates, we may elect to pursue relationships with other CMOs for manufacturing clinical supplies for later-stage trials and for commercialization.&#160; We do not own or operate manufacturing facilities for the production of clinical quantities of our product candidates.&#160; We currently have no plans to build our own clinical or commercial scale manufacturing capabilities.&#160; To meet our projected needs for clinical supplies to support our activities through regulatory approval and commercial</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">36<a name="PB_36_121734_2897"></a></font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">manufacturing, the CMOs with whom we currently work will need to increase scale of production or we will need to secure alternate suppliers.&#160; We have not currently qualified alternate suppliers in the event the current CMOs we utilize are unable to scale production.&#160; Although we rely on CMOs, we have personnel with pharmaceutical development and manufacturing experience who are responsible for the relationships with our CMOs.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Employees</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, we had 62 full-time employees, 17 of whom hold Ph.D. or M.D. degrees.&#160; Of these full-time employees, 49 were directly engaged in development activities, with the remainder serving in primarily general and administrative capacities.&#160; None of our employees are represented by labor unions or covered by collective bargaining agreements.&#160; We consider our relationship with our employees to be good.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Research and Development</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have dedicated a significant portion of our resources to our efforts to develop our product candidates, particularly rolapitant.&#160; We incurred research and development expenses, including acquired in-process research and development, of $12.3 million, $55.2 million, and $77.7 million during the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013, respectively.&#160; We anticipate that a significant portion of our operating expenses will continue to be related to research and development in 2014 as we continue to advance our product candidates through clinical development and incur up-front, milestone and other costs under our immuno&#151;oncology collaboration with AnaptysBio.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Available Information</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our internet website address is http://www.tesarobio.com.&#160; Through our website, we make available, free of charge, our annual reports on Form&nbsp;10-K, quarterly reports on Form&nbsp;10-Q, current reports on Form&nbsp;8-K, any amendments to those reports, proxy and registration statements, and all of our insider Section&nbsp;16 reports, as soon as reasonably practicable after such material is electronically filed with, or furnished to, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, or the SEC.&#160; These SEC reports can be accessed through the &#147;Investors&#148; section of our website.&#160; The information found on our website is not part of this or any other report we file with, or furnish to, the SEC.&#160; Paper copies of our SEC reports are available free of charge upon request in writing to Investor Relations, TESARO,&nbsp;Inc., 1000 Winter Street, Suite&nbsp;3300, Waltham, MA 02451.&#160; The content on any website referred to in this Form&nbsp;10-K is not incorporated by reference into this Form&nbsp;10-K unless expressly noted.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We currently operate in one segment.&#160; For additional information regarding our financial results, including measures of our accumulated deficit and information on our assets, refer the Notes to Financial Statements included in Part&nbsp;II,&nbsp;Item 8, &#147;Financial Statements and Supplementary Data&#148;, of this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K.</font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS</font></b><a name="Item1a_RiskFactors_162818"></a></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Investing in our common stock involves a high degree of risk.&#160; You should carefully consider the following discussion of risk factors, in its entirety, in addition to the other information contained in this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K, including our financial statements and the related notes.&#160; We cannot assure you that any of the events discussed in the risk factors below will not occur.&#160; These risks, or other events that we do not currently anticipate or that we currently deem immaterial, may have a material adverse effect on our business, prospects, financial condition and results of operations.</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Risks Related to Our Financial Position and Capital Needs</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">We have incurred significant losses since our inception and anticipate that we will continue to incur losses in the future.</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are a development stage biopharmaceutical company with a limited operating history.&#160; Investment in biopharmaceutical product development is highly speculative because it entails substantial upfront capital expenditures and significant risk that a product candidate will fail to gain regulatory approval or become commercially viable.&#160; We have not generated any revenue from product sales to date, and we continue to incur significant development and other expenses related to our ongoing operations.&#160; As a result, we are not profitable and have incurred losses in each period since our inception in 2010.&#160; For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, we reported a net loss of $92.4&nbsp;million and had a deficit accumulated during the development stage of $179.5 million as of December&nbsp;31, 2013.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We expect to continue to incur losses for the foreseeable future, and we expect these losses to increase as we continue our development of, and seek regulatory approvals for, our product candidates, and begin to commercialize any approved products.&#160; We may encounter unforeseen expenses, difficulties, complications, delays and other unknown factors that may adversely affect our business.&#160; The size of our future net losses will depend, in part, on the rate of future growth of our expenses, our ability to generate revenues and the timing and amount of milestones and other required payments to third parties.&#160; If any of our product candidates fail in clinical trials or do not gain regulatory approval, or if approved, fail to achieve market acceptance, we may never become profitable.&#160; Even if we achieve profitability in the future, we may not be able to sustain profitability in subsequent periods.&#160; Our prior losses and expected future losses have had, and will continue to have, an adverse effect on our stockholders&#146; equity and working capital.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">We have a very limited operating history, which may make it difficult for you to evaluate the success of our business to date and to assess our future viability.</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We were incorporated in March&nbsp;2010.&#160; Our operations to date have been limited to organizing and staffing our company, acquiring product and technology rights, and conducting product development activities for our three current product candidates.&#160; We have not yet obtained regulatory approval for, or demonstrated an ability to commercialize, any of our product candidates.&#160; Consequently, any predictions about our future success, performance or viability may not be as accurate as they could be if we had a longer operating history, approved products on the market, or both.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">We currently have no source of revenue and may never become profitable.</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To date, we have not generated any revenues from the three clinical-stage product candidates that we have in-licensed, rolapitant, niraparib and TSR-011.&#160; We also have not generated any revenues from the other product candidates that we have in-licensed, TSR-042, our monospecific antibody product candidates targeting TIM-3 and LAG-3 and our dual-reactive antibody product candidates targeting PD-1/TIM-3 and PD-1/LAG-3, which are in preclinical development.&#160; Our ability to generate revenue and become profitable depends upon our ability to successfully commercialize products, including any of our three clinical-stage product candidates, or the other product candidates that we have or may in-license or acquire in the future.&#160; Even if we are able to successfully achieve regulatory approval for rolapitant, niraparib, TSR-011 or TSR-042, we do not know when any of these products will generate revenue for us, if at all.&#160; Our ability to generate revenue from our current or future product candidates also depends on a number of additional factors, including our ability to:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">successfully complete development activities, including clinical trials for rolapitant, niraparib and TSR-011;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">complete and submit new drug applications, or NDAs or biologic license applications, or BLAs, to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, or FDA, and obtain regulatory approval for indications for which there is a commercial market;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">complete and submit applications to, and obtain regulatory approval from, foreign regulatory authorities;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">set a commercially viable price for our products;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">38<a name="PB_38_121734_7748"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">obtain commercial quantities of rolapitant, niraparib, TSR-011 and any of our other product candidates at acceptable cost levels;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">develop a commercial organization capable of sales, marketing and distribution in our core strategic markets;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">find suitable partners to help us market, sell and distribute our approved products in non-core markets; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">obtain adequate reimbursement from third-party payors, including government payors.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition, because of the numerous risks and uncertainties associated with product development, including the risk that our product candidates may not advance through development or achieve the endpoints of applicable clinical trials, we are unable to predict the timing or amount of increased expenses, or when or if we will be able to achieve or maintain profitability.&#160; Even if we are able to complete the process described above, we anticipate incurring significant costs associated with commercializing these products.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Even if we are able to generate revenues from the sale of our products, we may not become profitable and may need to obtain additional funding to continue operations.&#160; If we fail to become profitable or are unable to sustain profitability on a continuing basis, then we may be unable to continue our operations at planned levels and be forced to reduce our operations.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">If we require additional capital to fund our operations and we fail to obtain necessary financing, we may be unable to complete the development and commercialization of our product candidates.</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our operations have consumed substantial amounts of cash since inception.&#160; We expect to continue to spend substantial amounts to advance the clinical development of our product candidates and launch and commercialize any product candidates for which we receive regulatory approval, including building our own commercial organizations to address certain markets.&#160; We believe that our existing cash and cash equivalents, together with proceeds from our February&nbsp;2014 follow-on stock offering, will be sufficient to fund our projected operating requirements through at least the next twelve months.&#160; However, we expect to require additional capital for the further development and commercialization of our product candidates and may also need to raise additional funds to pursue our strategy of in-licensing or acquiring additional product candidates.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Until we can generate a sufficient amount of revenue from our products, if ever, we expect to finance future cash needs through additional public or private equity or debt offerings </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">and may seek additional capital through arrangements with strategic partners </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">or from other sources.&#160; Additional capital may not be available on reasonable terms, if at all.&#160; If we are unable to raise additional capital in sufficient amounts or on terms acceptable to us, we may have to significantly delay, scale back or discontinue the development or commercialization of one or more of our product candidates.&#160; Raising additional funds through the issuance of debt or equity securities could result in dilution to our existing stockholders, increased fixed payment obligations, or both.&#160; Furthermore, these securities may have rights senior to those of our common stock and could contain covenants that would restrict our operations and potentially impair our competitiveness, such as limitations on our ability to incur additional debt, limitations on our ability to acquire, sell or license intellectual property rights and other operating restrictions that could adversely impact our ability to conduct our business.&#160; Any of these events could significantly harm our business, financial condition and prospects.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our forecast of the period of time through which our financial resources will be adequate to support our operations is a forward-looking statement and involves risks and uncertainties, and actual results could vary as a result of a number of factors, including the factors discussed elsewhere in this &#147;Risk Factors&#148; section.&#160; We have based this estimate on assumptions that may prove to be wrong, and we could utilize our available capital resources sooner than we currently expect.&#160; Our future funding requirements, both short and long-term, will depend on many factors, including:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the initiation, progress, timing, costs and results of clinical trials for our current product candidates and any future product candidates we may in-license, including our Phase&nbsp;3 clinical trials for rolapitant and niraparib and our Phase 1/2a clinical trial for TSR-011;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the preclinical and clinical development plans we and our collaborator, AnaptysBio,&nbsp;Inc., or AnaptysBio, establish for our monospecific antibody product candidates targeting PD-1 (TSR-042), TIM-3 and LAG-3 and our dual-reactive antibody product candidates targeting PD-1/TIM-3 and PD-1/LAG-3;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the attainment of milestones and our obligations to make milestone payments, royalty payments, or both to OPKO Health,&nbsp;Inc., or OPKO, Merck Sharpe&nbsp;&amp; Dohme Corp., a subsidiary of Merck&nbsp;&amp; Co.,&nbsp;Inc., or Merck,</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">39<a name="PB_39_121734_141"></a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Amgen,&nbsp;Inc., or Amgen, or AnaptysBio or to any other future product candidate licensor, if any, under our in-licensing agreements;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the number and characteristics of product candidates that we in-license and develop;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the outcome, timing and cost of regulatory approvals by the FDA and comparable foreign regulatory authorities, including the potential that the FDA or comparable foreign regulatory authorities may require that we perform more studies than those that we currently expect;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the cost of filing, prosecuting, defending and enforcing any patent claims and other intellectual property rights;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the effect of competing technological and market developments;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the cost and timing of completion of commercial-scale outsourced manufacturing activities; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the cost of establishing sales, marketing and distribution capabilities for rolapitant or any product candidates for which we may receive regulatory approval.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">If we lack the capital to expand our operations or otherwise capitalize on our business opportunities, our business, financial condition and results of operations could be materially adversely affected.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Risks Related to Our Business and Industry</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Our future success is dependent primarily on the regulatory approval and commercialization of our product candidates, including rolapitant, which is currently undergoing Phase&nbsp;3 clinical trials.</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We currently do not have any products that have gained regulatory approval.&#160; The success of our business is dependent upon our ability to develop and commercialize our product candidates, particularly rolapitant, niraparib and TSR-011, which are currently our only clinical-stage product candidates.&#160; We are particularly dependent on the future success of rolapitant, because it is our most advanced product candidate.&#160; Our other product candidates are at earlier stages of development.&#160; Niraparib is currently in a Phase&nbsp;3 clinical trial in ovarian cancer patients which commenced during 2013, and TSR-011 is still early in clinical development.&#160; Our monospecific antibody product candidates targeting PD-1 (TSR-042), TIM-3 and LAG-3 and our dual-reactive antibody product candidates targeting PD-1/TIM-3 and PD-1/LAG-3 are in preclinical development.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As a result, our business is substantially dependent on our ability to complete the development of, obtain regulatory approval for, and successfully commercialize rolapitant (and to a lesser degree, our other product candidates) in a timely manner.&#160; We cannot commercialize product candidates in the United States without first obtaining regulatory approval for the product from the FDA; similarly, we cannot commercialize product candidates outside of the United States without obtaining regulatory approval from comparable foreign regulatory authorities.&#160; Before obtaining regulatory approvals for the commercial sale of any product candidate for a target indication, we must demonstrate with substantial evidence gathered in preclinical and well-controlled clinical studies, and, with respect to approval in the United States, to the satisfaction of the FDA, that the product candidate is safe and effective for use for that target indication and that the manufacturing facilities, processes and controls are adequate.&#160; The process to develop, obtain regulatory approval for and commercialize product candidates is long, complex and costly both inside and outside of the United States.&#160; Even if rolapitant were to successfully obtain approval from the FDA and comparable foreign regulatory authorities, any approval might contain significant limitations, including use restrictions for certain patient populations; warnings, precautions or contraindications; or may be subject to burdensome post-approval study or risk management requirements.&#160; If we are unable to obtain regulatory approval for rolapitant in one or more jurisdictions, or if any approval contains significant limitations, we may not be able to obtain sufficient funding or generate sufficient revenue to continue the development of our existing product candidates or any other product candidate that we may in-license or acquire in the future.&#160; Furthermore, even if we obtain approval for rolapitant from the FDA and comparable foreign regulatory authorities, we will still need to develop a commercial organization, establish commercially viable pricing and obtain adequate reimbursement from third-party and government payors.&#160; If we are unable to successfully commercialize rolapitant, we may not be able to earn sufficient revenues to continue our business.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">40<a name="PB_40_121734_7608"></a></font></p>
