<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">an increase of $6.8 million in salaries, benefits and other personnel costs (including stock-based compensation) related to increased research and development headcount supporting the growth of our development activities.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">General and Administrative Expenses.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; General and administrative expenses were $14.8 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, compared to $6.7 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, an increase of $8.1 million.&#160; The increase was due primarily to increases of $4.5&nbsp;million in stock-based compensation expense, of which $1.8 million was related to accounting for awards held by a non-employee consultant, with the remainder related to increased awards of stock options to both new and existing employees and higher grant-date fair values of those awards, $2.1 million in salaries, benefits and other personnel related costs, and $1.5&nbsp;million in professional and consulting fees and other expenses to support corporate operational activities, such as insurance and certain other costs associated with public company operations.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Acquired In-Process Research and Development Expenses.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;We recorded $1.9 million in acquired in-process research and development expenses during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, representing a milestone payment made as the result of the first patient dosing in the Phase 3 clinical trial of niraparib in patients with high grade serous, platinum sensitive, relapsed ovarian cancer, which occurred in July&nbsp;2013.&nbsp; During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, acquired in-process research and development expenses totaled $8.0 million, consisting of a $7.0 million up-front cash payment associated with the May&nbsp;2012 acquisition of licensing rights for our niraparib program, and a $1.0 million milestone payment in November&nbsp;2012 relating to our TSR-011 program.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Other Income (Expense), Net</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.&nbsp;&nbsp;Other income for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 and 2013 consisted solely of interest income earned on cash and cash equivalents.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Comparison of the Year Ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 to the Year Ended December&nbsp;31, 2011</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Revenues.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; We did not recognize any revenue for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011 or 2012.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Research and Development Expenses</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.&#160; Research and development expenses were $47.2&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, compared to $11.8 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, an increase of $35.4&nbsp;million.&#160; The increase was primarily due to expenses related to the development of our in-licensed product candidates, rolapitant and TSR-011, as well as increases in the personnel and related costs necessary to support the progress of our clinical development activities.&#160; Significant 2012 activities causing the increase in expense included:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">an increase of $27.5 million in costs associated with rolapitant clinical trials and the Phase&nbsp;3 clinical program, including drug product development, clinical supply manufacturing and distribution;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">an increase of $2.4&nbsp;million associated with product development and IND enabling studies for TSR-011, which was acquired in March&nbsp;2011;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">an increase of $0.7 million in product research and development activities for niraparib, which was acquired in May&nbsp;2012; and</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">76<a name="PB_76_105836_7906"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">an increase of $4.8 million primarily for salaries, benefits and other personnel costs to support the growth of our development activities.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">General and Administrative Expenses.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;General and administrative expenses for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 were $6.7&nbsp;million compared to $3.2 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, an increase of $3.5 million.&#160; The increase was due primarily to an increase of $1.8&nbsp;million in salaries, benefits and other personnel related costs and $1.7&nbsp;million in professional and consulting fees and other expenses to support corporate operational activities, including certain additional costs associated with public company operations.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Acquired In-Process Research and Development Expenses.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;We had acquired in-process research and development expenses of $8.0 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, compared to $0.5&nbsp;million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011.&#160; The increase was due to the difference in up-front acquisition costs and milestone payments associated with our obtaining and maintaining licensing rights for different products during these time periods.&#160; We paid $7.0 million in cash and recognized the entire amount as acquired in-process research and development expense to acquire the licensing rights to our niraparib program, and we paid a $1.0 million milestone to Amgen related to our TSR-011 program during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012.&#160; We paid $0.5&nbsp;million, which was recognized as acquired in-process research and development expense, to acquire the licensing rights to our TSR-011 program in the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Other Income (Expense), Net.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;Other income, net, was $0.2 million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, compared to other expense, net, of $(1.0) million for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, an increase of $1.2 million.&#160; The increase was primarily due to the change in value of the Series&nbsp;A-2 Purchase Rights issued in connection with the issuance of 10,000,000 shares of Series&nbsp;A-1 convertible preferred stock on May&nbsp;10, 2010.&#160; The Company recorded the fair value of the Series&nbsp;A-2 Purchase Rights at the date of issuance of the Series&nbsp;A-1 convertible preferred stock and adjusted the carrying value of such rights to their estimated fair value at each reporting date as well as upon settlement.&#160; On February&nbsp;10, 2011, the holders of the Series&nbsp;A-2 Purchase Rights exercised such rights.&#160; From January&nbsp;1, 2011 to February&nbsp;10, 2011, the estimated increase in fair value of the Series&nbsp;A-2 Purchase Rights resulted in other expense of $1.0&nbsp;million.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Liquidity and Capital Resources</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Sources of Liquidity</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">To date, we have not generated any revenue.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, our principal source of liquidity was cash and cash equivalents, which totaled $130.3 million.&#160; Since our inception on March&nbsp;26, 2010, we have funded our operations primarily through the private placement of our equity securities, our initial public offering, and follow-on common stock offerings.&#160; On July&nbsp;3, 2012, we completed our initial public offering through which we sold 6,000,000 shares of common stock at a price of $13.50 per share.&#160; On July&nbsp;23, 2012, the underwriters purchased an additional 430,183 shares by exercising a portion of the over-allotment option granted to them in connection with the initial public offering.&#160; As a result of the closing of the initial public offering and subsequent exercise of the over-allotment option, we received aggregate net proceeds of approximately $78.0 million, which is net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses.&#160; In March&nbsp;2013, we sold </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5,428,000 shares of common stock in an underwritten public offering pursuant to a registration statement on Form&nbsp;S-1, at a price of $18.00 per share, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $91.3 million</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">, which is net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In February&nbsp;2014, we sold </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">3,200,000 shares of common stock in an underwritten public offering pursuant to a registration statement on Form&nbsp;S-3, at a price of $31.50 per share, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $94.1 million</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">, which is net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">. </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March 2014, we entered into a collaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio, under which we are obligated to make an up-front, non-creditable and non-refundable cash payment of $17.0 million to AnaptysBio.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Prior to our initial public offering, we had received $120.4&nbsp;million in net proceeds from the private placement of our convertible preferred stock. &nbsp;This amount includes net proceeds of approximately $58.3 million that we received in March&nbsp;2012 upon the issuance of 26,884,442 shares of our Series&nbsp;B convertible preferred stock to certain existing investors in connection with the Series&nbsp;B Preferred Stock Purchase Agreement.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Cash Flows</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table presents a summary of the primary sources and uses of cash for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013 (in thousands):</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">  </font></p>

<td width="13%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:13.38%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">37,292</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.62%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>    </td>
<td width="13%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:13.38%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">85,620</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border:none;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.62%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">$</font></p>    </td>
<td width="13%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;border-bottom:double windowtext 2.25pt;border-left:none;border-right:none;border-top:solid windowtext 1.0pt;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:13.38%;">
<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">4,865</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.26%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>  </table>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Operating Activities.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; The use of cash in all periods resulted primarily from our net losses, adjusted for non-cash charges and changes in operating assets and liabilities.&#160; The significant increases in cash used in operating activities for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 and 2012 were primarily due to increases in research and development expenses as we continued to develop our product candidates.&#160; These increases in research and development expenses were primarily caused by increases in external research and development costs and in headcount.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Amounts recorded in the consolidated statements of operations as &#147;Acquired in-process research and development&#148; are included in the consolidated statements of cash flows as adjustments to reconcile net loss to net cash used in operating activities because these amounts are charged to expense as incurred in the respective periods, but the cash payments relating to these expenses are classified as investing activities in the consolidated statements of cash flows, as described below.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Investing Activities.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Cash used in investing activities primarily consisted of the following amounts relating to the acquisition of product candidate licenses and milestone payments, all of which were recorded as acquired in-process research and development expense as incurred:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, a $0.5 million up-front payment for the TSR-011 program license;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, a $7.0 million up-front payment for the niraparib program license and a $1.0 million development milestone payment related to the TSR-011 program; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, a $1.9 million development milestone payment related to the niraparib program.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash used in investing activities for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013 also included the use of $0.1 million, $0.2 million, and $0.4 million, respectively, for purchases of property and equipment.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Financing Activities.</font></i><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Cash provided by financing activities primarily consisted of the following amounts raised in issuances of equity instruments:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, net cash proceeds of $10.0 million from the issuance of Series&nbsp;A-2 convertible preferred stock, and $42.1 million from the issuance of Series&nbsp;B convertible preferred stock;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012, net cash proceeds of $58.3 million from the issuance of Series&nbsp;B convertible preferred stock, and $78.0 million </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses) </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">from our initial public offering of common stock and the related partial exercise by the underwriters of the over-allotment option granted to them in connection with the initial public offering; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, net cash proceeds of $91.3 million (net of underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses) from a follow-on offering of common stock.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Cash provided by financing activities during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 also included $0.8 million in proceeds from exercises of stock options under our 2010 Stock Incentive Plan and 2012 Omnibus Incentive Plan, and issuances of common stock under our 2012 Employee Stock Purchase Plan.&#160; No cash was provided by these equity plans during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, and cash provided by these equity plans during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 was not significant.</font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Operating Capital Requirements</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We do not anticipate commercializing any of our product candidates within the next twelve months.&#160; Further, we anticipate that we will continue to generate losses for the foreseeable future, and we expect the losses to primarily increase as we continue the development of, and seek regulatory approvals for, our product candidates, and begin to commercialize any approved products.&#160; We are subject to all of the risks incident in the development of new biopharmaceutical products, and we may encounter unforeseen expenses, difficulties, complications, delays and other unknown factors that may adversely affect our business and cause increased uses of cash.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We believe that our existing cash and cash equivalents, including the proceeds from our February&nbsp;2014 follow-on stock offering, will be sufficient to fund our projected operating requirements, including costs relating to our March 2014 collaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio, through at least the next twelve months.&#160; However, we expect to require additional capital for the further development and commercialization of our product candidates and may also need to raise additional funds to pursue our strategy of in-licensing or acquiring additional product candidates.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Until we can generate a sufficient amount of revenue from our products, if ever, we expect to finance future cash needs through additional public or private equity or debt offerings and may seek additional capital through arrangements with strategic partners or from other sources.&#160; Additional capital may not be available on reasonable terms, if at all.&#160; If we are unable to raise additional capital in sufficient amounts or on terms acceptable to us, we may have to significantly delay, scale back or discontinue the development or commercialization of one or more of our product candidates.&#160; Raising additional funds through the issuance of debt or equity securities could result in dilution to our existing stockholders, increased fixed payment obligations, or both.&#160; Furthermore, these securities may have rights senior to those of our common stock and could contain covenants that would restrict our operations and potentially impair our competitiveness, such as limitations on our ability to incur additional debt, limitations on our ability to acquire, sell or license intellectual property rights and other operating restrictions that could adversely impact our ability to conduct our business.&#160; Any of these events could significantly harm our business, financial condition and prospects.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our forecast of the period of time through which our financial resources will be adequate to support our operations is a forward-looking statement and involves risks and uncertainties, and actual results could vary as a result of a number of factors.&#160; We have based this estimate on assumptions that may prove to be wrong, and we could utilize our available capital resources sooner than we currently expect.&#160; Our future funding requirements, both short and long-term, will depend on many factors, including:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the initiation, progress, timing, costs and results of clinical trials for our current product candidates and any future product candidates we may in-license, including our Phase&nbsp;3 clinical trials for rolapitant and niraparib;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the clinical development plans we establish for TSR-011;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the discovery, preclinical and clinical development plans that are or will be established for potential product candidates under our collaboration with AnaptysBio;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the attainment of milestones and our obligations to make milestone payments, royalty payments, or both to OPKO, Merck, Amgen or AnaptysBio, or to any other future product candidate licensor, if any, under our in-licensing agreements;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the number and characteristics of product candidates that we in-license and develop;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the outcome, timing and cost of regulatory approvals by the FDA and comparable foreign regulatory authorities, including the potential that the FDA or comparable foreign regulatory authorities may require that we perform more studies than those that we currently expect;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the cost of filing, prosecuting, defending and enforcing any patent claims and other intellectual property rights;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the effect of competing technological and market developments;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the cost and timing of completion of commercial-scale outsourced manufacturing activities; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">79<a name="PB_79_105836_8715"></a></font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">the cost of establishing sales, marketing and distribution capabilities for rolapitant or any product candidates for which we may receive regulatory approval.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">If we lack the capital to expand our operations or otherwise capitalize on our business opportunities, our business, financial condition and results of operations could be materially adversely affected.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Contractual Obligations and Commitments</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table summarizes our contractual obligations as of December&nbsp;31, 2013 (in thousands):</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.78%;">

<p align="right" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:right;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#151;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.3%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>  </table>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Operating and Facility Lease Obligations</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Operating lease obligations in the table above relate to an operating lease for an office facility.&#160; We lease approximately 23,800 square feet of office space in Waltham, Massachusetts under a non-cancelable operating lease agreement.&#160; The term of the lease commenced April&nbsp;1, 2013 and continues through March&nbsp;31, 2015.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In January&nbsp;2014, we entered into an amendment of the lease agreement whereby beginning in March&nbsp;2014, we expanded the total leased space in the facility to 53,200 square feet and extended the term of the lease through June&nbsp;30, 2017.&#160; The amended lease provides for additional rent expense of approximately $940,000 on an annualized basis.&#160; In addition, the amended lease increased the security deposit to approximately $0.7 million and continues to require us to pay a proportionate share of certain of the landlord&#146;s annual operating costs.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Purchase Commitments</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Purchase commitments in the table above relate to agreements with certain vendors for the provision of services, including services related to data management and clinical operation support services, that we are not contractually able to terminate for convenience and avoid any and all future obligations to the vendors.&nbsp; Under such agreements, we are contractually obligated to make certain minimum payments to the vendors, with the exact amounts in the event of termination to be based on the timing of the termination and the exact terms of the agreement.&#160; In the table above, we have included our estimated commitments under such agreements as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, assuming we do not terminate these agreements.&#160; The actual amounts we pay in the future to the vendors under such agreements may be less than the amounts shown in the table above.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, we had several ongoing clinical studies in various clinical trial stages.&#160; Our most significant clinical trial expenditures are made to clinical research organizations, or CROs.&#160; As a result of cancellation rights included in our contractual agreements with our CROs (other than those described in the preceding paragraph), we have not included any amounts related to our CRO contracts in the contractual obligations table above.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Product Licenses</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In addition to the amounts set forth in the table above, we have certain obligations under licensing agreements with third parties that are contingent upon achieving various development, regulatory and commercial milestones.&#160; Pursuant to our license agreement with OPKO for the development and commercialization of rolapitant, we are required to make milestone payments to OPKO of up to an aggregate of $30.0&nbsp;million if certain regulatory approvals and initial commercial sales milestones are achieved.&#160; Further, we are required to make additional milestone payments to OPKO of up to an aggregate of $85.0&nbsp;million if specified levels of annual net sales of rolapitant are achieved.&#160; Pursuant to our license agreement with Merck for the development and commercialization of niraparib, we have made one milestone payment of $1.9 million to date, and we are required to make total milestone payments to Merck of up to $57.0&nbsp;million in development and regulatory milestones for the first indication, up to $29.5&nbsp;million in development and regulatory milestones for each successive indication, and up to $87.5&nbsp;million in one-time sales milestones based on the achievement of annual sales objectives.&#160; Pursuant to our license</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">80<a name="PB_80_105836_563"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">agreement with Amgen for the development and commercialization of TSR-011, we have made one milestone payment of $1.0 million to date, and we are required to make total milestone payments to Amgen of up to an aggregate of $138.0&nbsp;million if specified clinical development, regulatory, initial commercialization and annual net product sales milestones are achieved. </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Pursuant to our March 2014 collaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio, </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">we are obligated to make an up-front, non-creditable and non-refundable cash payment of $17.0 million to AnaptysBio.&#160; We are also</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;"> required to </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">reimburse AnaptysBio on a quarterly basis for specified costs incurred by AnaptysBio in its initial discovery and development activities covered by the agreement.&#160; For each of our three development programs, we will also be required to make milestone payments to AnaptysBio of up to $18.0 million if certain research and development milestone events are achieved, up to an additional $90.0 million of milestone payments if certain U.S. and non-U.S. regulatory submissions and approvals occur in initial and subsequent indications, and additional commercial milestone payments if specified levels of annual net sales of a product are attained.</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#160; Finally, pursuant to the terms of each of these license agreements, when and if commercial sales of a product commence, we will pay royalties to our licensors on net sales of the respective products.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">Technology Licenses</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In October&nbsp;2012, we entered into two license agreements with AstraZeneca UK Limited, under which we made aggregate upfront payments of $0.4 million.&#160; These agreements provide us with the exclusive right to certain methods of treating patients with PARP inhibitors solely with respect to niraparib.&#160; Under certain circumstances, we may be required to make milestone and royalty payments to AstraZeneca UK Limited based on the achievement of certain development and regulatory milestone events with regard to niraparib, and on net sales of niraparib.&#160; We have not included any amounts related to these agreements in the table above.&#160; We made milestone payments related to these agreements totaling $0.2 million to AstraZeneca during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Off-Balance Sheet Arrangements</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, we did not have any off-balance sheet arrangements as defined in Regulation S-K,&nbsp;Item 303(a)(4)(ii).</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Recently Issued Accounting Pronouncements</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2013, the FASB issued Accounting Standards Update, or ASU, No.&nbsp;2013-05, which requires a parent company to release any related cumulative translation adjustment into net income only if a sale or transfer results in the complete or substantially complete liquidation of the foreign entity in which the subsidiary or group of assets had resided.&nbsp; This guidance is effective prospectively for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2013.&nbsp; Early adoption and retrospective application are permitted.&nbsp; We expect to adopt this guidance effective January&nbsp;1, 2014 and do not expect this adoption to have a material effect on our consolidated financial statements.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In July&nbsp;2013, the FASB issued ASU No.&nbsp;2013-11, which requires entities to present an unrecognized tax benefit, or a portion of an unrecognized tax benefit, as a reduction to a deferred tax asset for a net operating loss carryforward or a similar tax loss or a tax credit carryforward, unless certain conditions exist.&nbsp; This guidance is effective prospectively for fiscal years beginning after December&nbsp;15, 2013.&nbsp; Early adoption and retrospective application are permitted.&nbsp; We expect to adopt this guidance effective January&nbsp;1, 2014 and do not expect this adoption to have a material effect on our consolidated financial statements.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 7A. QUANTITATIVE AND QUALITATIVE DISCLOSURES ABOUT MARKET RISK</font></b><a name="Item7a_QuantitativeAndQualitative_164738"></a></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We are exposed to market risk related to changes in interest rates.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2012 and December&nbsp;31, 2013, we had cash and cash equivalents of $125.4 million and $130.3 million, respectively, consisting primarily of money market funds.&#160; Our primary exposure to market risk is interest rate sensitivity, which is affected by changes in the general level of United States interest rates, particularly because our investments are in short-term securities.&#160; Our securities are subject to interest rate risk and may fall in value if market interest rates increase.&#160; Due to the short-term duration of our investment portfolio and the low-risk profile of our investments, an immediate 100 basis point change in interest rates would not have a material effect on the fair market value of our portfolio.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">81<a name="PB_81_105836_9497"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm</font></b><a name="ReportOfIndependentRegisteredPubl_165148"></a></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Board of Directors and Stockholders</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">TESARO,&nbsp;Inc.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We have audited the accompanying consolidated balance sheets of TESARO,&nbsp;Inc. (a development stage enterprise) (the &#147;Company&#148;) as of December&nbsp;31, 2012 and 2013, and the related consolidated statements of operations and comprehensive loss, convertible preferred stock and stockholders&#146; (deficit) equity, and cash flows for each of the three years in the period ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, and for the period from March&nbsp;26, 2010 (inception) to December&nbsp;31, 2013.&#160; These financial statements are the responsibility of the Company&#146;s management.&#160; Our responsibility is to express an opinion on these financial statements based on our audits.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">We conducted our audits in accordance with the standards of the Public Company Accounting Oversight Board (United States).&#160; Those standards require that we plan and perform the audit to obtain reasonable assurance about whether the financial statements are free of material misstatement.&#160; We were not engaged to perform an audit of the Company&#146;s internal control over financial reporting.&#160; Our audits included consideration of internal control over financial reporting as a basis for designing audit procedures that are appropriate in the circumstances, but not for the purpose of expressing an opinion on the effectiveness of the Company&#146;s internal control over financial reporting.&#160; Accordingly, we express no such opinion.&#160; An audit also includes examining, on a test basis, evidence supporting the amounts and disclosures in the financial statements, assessing the accounting principles used and significant estimates made by management, and evaluating the overall financial statement presentation.&#160; We believe that our audits provide a reasonable basis for our opinion.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In our opinion, the financial statements referred to above present fairly, in all material respects, the consolidated financial position of TESARO,&nbsp;Inc. as of December&nbsp;31, 2012 and 2013, and the consolidated results of its operations and its cash flows for each of the three years in the period ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, and for the period from March&nbsp;26, 2010 (inception) to December&nbsp;31, 2013, in conformity with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">TESARO,&nbsp;Inc.</font></b></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">(A Development Stage Company)</font></b></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Consolidated Statements of Convertible Preferred Stock and Stockholders&#146; (Deficit) Equity</font></b><a name="ConsolidatedStatementsOfConvertib_163641"></a></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">&nbsp;</font></i></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;">(all amounts in 000&#146;s, except share and per share data)</font></i></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<table border="0" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0" width="100%" style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100.0%;">

<td width="26%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:26.0%;">

<td width="10%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:10.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><b><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;font-weight:bold;">&nbsp;</font></b></p>    </td>

<td width="7%" colspan="2" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:7.0%;">

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 20.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Conversion of convertible preferred stock to   common stock</font></p>    </td>
<td width="2%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:2.54%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
