<tr>
<td width="44%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:44.42%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Volatility</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.14%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">67% - 68%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.14%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">66% - 71%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="14%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.98%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">73% - 78%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.2%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>
<tr>
<td width="44%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:44.42%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Risk-free interest rate</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.14%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.07% - 2.03%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.14%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">0.89% - 1.56%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="14%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.98%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">1.07% - 2.05%</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" bgcolor="#CCEEFF" style="background:#CCEEFF;padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.2%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>
<tr>
<td width="44%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:44.42%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt 10.0pt;text-indent:-10.0pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Expected term (years)</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.14%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">6.25</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.14%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="15%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:15.0%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">6.25</font></p>    </td>
<td width="3%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:3.12%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>
<td width="14%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:14.98%;">
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">5.50 - 6.25</font></p>    </td>
<td width="1%" valign="bottom" style="padding:0in 0in 0in 0in;width:1.2%;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:1.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>    </td>   </tr>  </table>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company uses the simplified method as prescribed by SEC Staff Accounting Bulletin No.&nbsp;107, <i>Share-Based Payment</i>, to calculate the expected term, as it does not have sufficient historical exercise data to provide a reasonable basis upon which to estimate the expected term for options granted to employees.&#160; The expected term is applied to all stock option grants as a whole, as the Company does not expect substantially different exercise or post-vesting termination behavior among its employee population.&#160; The computation of expected volatility is based on the historical volatility of a representative group of public biotechnology and life sciences companies with similar characteristics to the Company, including early stage of product development and therapeutic focus.&#160; The risk-free interest rate is based on a treasury instrument whose term is consistent with the expected term of the stock options.&#160; Management assesses expected forfeitures based on the experience of the Company coupled with comparison to data from the representative group of company peers and recognizes compensation costs only for those equity awards expected to vest.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">96<a name="PB_96_111655_9801"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company recorded stock-based compensation expense associated with employee stock options of $281,000, $1,526,000, and $6,770,000 for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013, respectively.&#160; The total for the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013 includes $1,132,000 related to accounting for awards held by a non-employee consultant.&#160; The weighted-average grant date fair values of options granted in the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013 were $2.63, $5.29 and $18.48 per share, respectively.&#160; The aggregate intrinsic value of options exercised during the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013 was $0, $0.2 million, and $4.8 million, respectively.&#160; The intrinsic value of a stock option is the amount by which the fair market value of the underlying stock on the exercise date exceeds the exercise price of the stock option.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">At December&nbsp;31, 2013, there was $17.0 million of total unrecognized compensation cost related to unvested stock options, which the Company expects to recognize over a remaining weighted-average period of 2.5 years.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In October&nbsp;2012 and June&nbsp;2013, and as provided for under the 2012 Incentive Plan, the Company issued 16,644 and 4,368 shares of common stock, respectively, with aggregate values of $0.3 million for both periods, to certain non-employee board members who elected to receive shares of common stock in lieu of cash, as payment of fees owed them for services as members of the Company&#146;s board of directors.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the three months ended September&nbsp;30, 2013, the Company&#146;s former Executive Vice President, Chief Financial Officer, Treasurer and Secretary, or the former CFO, resigned from his employment with the Company effective August&nbsp;31, 2013 and transitioned to serving the Company as a non-employee consultant.&nbsp; In accordance with the terms of the 2012 Incentive Plan and the 2010 Incentive Plan, stock awards previously granted to the former CFO under these plans will continue to vest through March&nbsp;31, 2014 (the term of the Consulting Agreement).&nbsp; As a result, beginning in September&nbsp;2013, the Company accounts for unvested stock awards previously granted to the former CFO as non-employee awards.&nbsp; The Company records stock-based compensation expense based on the fair values of awards as measured on their vesting dates, and the fair values of any unvested awards are remeasured at each financial reporting date until they vest, with any increases or decreases in fair value recorded as stock-based compensation expense.&nbsp; Fair values of stock options are determined on each measurement date using the Black-Scholes option pricing model, and fair values of restricted stock awards are equal to the fair market value of the Company&#146;s common stock on the measurement date.&nbsp; During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company recorded incremental stock-based compensation expense of $1.8 million associated with these awards, as the result of the change in status of the former CFO.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Employee Stock Purchase Plan</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On June&nbsp;6, 2012, the board of directors adopted the 2012 ESPP, and the stockholders approved it on June&nbsp;18, 2012, to be effective in connection with the closing of the Company&#146;s initial public offering.&#160; A total of 275,000 shares of common stock were originally approved for future issuance under the 2012 ESPP pursuant to purchase rights granted to the Company&#146;s employees or to employees of the Company&#146;s designated subsidiaries.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, 267,169 shares remained available for issuance.&#160; The 2012 ESPP provides for consecutive six-month offering periods, during which participating employees may elect to have a portion of their compensation withheld and used for the purchase of common stock at the end of each offering period.&#160; The purchase price is equal to 85% of the lower of the fair market value of a share of common stock on the first trading date of each offering period or the fair market value of a share of common stock on the last trading day of the offering period, and is limited by participant to $25,000 in fair value of common stock per year.&#160; The 2012 ESPP will terminate on June&nbsp;6, 2022, the tenth anniversary of the date of initial adoption of the plan.&#160; For the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company issued a total of 7,831 shares of common stock under the 2012 ESPP and recognized $67,000 in related stock-based compensation expense.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Due to its operating losses in all periods to date, the Company has not recorded any tax benefits associated with stock-based compensation expense and option exercises.&#160; Tax benefits will be recorded when realized.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">7. Income Taxes</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company accounts for income taxes in accordance with ASC 740,&nbsp;<i>Income Taxes</i>, or ASC 740.&#160; Deferred income tax assets and liabilities are determined based upon temporary differences between the financial reporting and tax bases of assets and liabilities and are measured using the enacted tax rates and laws that will be in effect when the differences are expected to reverse.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">97<a name="PB_97_111655_2440"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='97',FILE='C:\JMS\107901\13-25458-1\task6621304\25458-1-fp.htm',USER='107901',CD='Mar 13 11:17 2014' -->

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">For the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2011, 2012 and 2013, the Company did not record any current or deferred income tax provisions or benefits.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company had federal net operating loss carryforwards of approximately $152.2&nbsp;million and state net operating loss carryforwards of $151.3&nbsp;million, which are available to reduce future taxable income.&#160; The Company also had federal research and development tax credit carryforwards of $3.1 million, which may be used to offset future tax liabilities.&#160; The net operating loss, or NOL, and tax credit carryforwards will expire at various dates through 2033.&#160; The NOL and tax credit carryforwards are subject to review and possible adjustment by the Internal Revenue Service, or the IRS, and state tax authorities.&#160; These carryforwards may become subject to an annual limitation in the event of certain cumulative changes in the ownership interest of significant shareholders over a three-year period in excess of 50%, as defined under Sections&nbsp;382 and 383 of the Internal Revenue Code, respectively, as well as similar state provisions.&#160; This could limit the amount of tax attributes that can be utilized annually to offset future taxable income or tax liabilities.&#160; The amount of the annual limitation is determined based on the value of the Company immediately prior to the ownership change.&#160; Subsequent ownership changes may further affect the limitation in future years.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s reserves related to taxes are based on a determination of whether and how much of a tax benefit taken by the Company in its tax filings or positions is more likely than not to be realized following resolution of any potential contingencies present related to the tax benefit.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company has recorded $0.5 million of gross unrecognized tax benefits related to research and development credits which, if recognized, would be offset by an adjustment to the valuation allowance.&#160; Thus, there would be no impact to the consolidated balance sheet or consolidated statement of operations if the benefit was recognized.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company&#146;s policy is to recognize both interest and penalties related to unrecognized tax benefits in income tax expense.&#160; Due to its historical net loss position, the Company has not recognized any interest or penalties related to unrecognized tax benefits.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The statute of limitations for assessment by the IRS and state tax authorities remains open for all tax years.&#160; The Company files a United States federal income tax return and a Commonwealth of Massachusetts state income tax return.&#160; There are currently no federal or state audits in process.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On January&nbsp;2, 2013, the President signed into law The American Taxpayer Relief Act of 2012, or ATRA.&nbsp; Under prior law, a taxpayer was entitled to a research tax credit for qualifying amounts paid or incurred on or before December&nbsp;31, 2011.&#160; ATRA extended the research credit for two years to December&nbsp;31, 2013.&nbsp; The extension of the research credit was retroactive and included amounts paid or incurred after December&nbsp;31, 2011.&nbsp; As a result of the retroactive extension, the Company recorded a deferred tax asset before valuation allowance of $0.9 million&nbsp;for qualifying amounts incurred in 2012.&nbsp; The deferred tax asset and corresponding valuation allowance were recorded in the period of enactment, which was the first quarter of 2013.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company recorded an additional deferred tax asset and corresponding valuation allowance of $1.5 million for qualifying amounts incurred in 2013.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table presents the principal components of the Company&#146;s deferred tax assets (in thousands):</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ASC 740 requires a valuation allowance to reduce the deferred tax assets reported if, based on the weight of available evidence, it is more likely than not that some portion or all of the deferred tax assets will not be realized.&#160; After consideration of all the evidence, both positive and negative, the Company has recorded full valuation allowances against its deferred tax assets at both December&nbsp;31, 2012 and 2013, because management has determined that is it more likely than not that these assets will not be realized.&#160; The increase in the valuation allowance in 2013 was related to the net loss incurred by the Company.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company had federal and state net operating losses of approximately $4.4 million related to excess tax deductions that have been excluded from the table above.&#160; The benefit of these net operating losses will be recognized as an increase in additional paid-in capital when it results in a reduction in taxable income.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table presents a reconciliation of income tax expense (benefit) at the statutory federal income tax rate to the effective income tax rate as reflected in the consolidated financial statements:</font></p>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">8. Employee Benefit Plan</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In 2010, the Company adopted a retirement plan, which is qualified under section&nbsp;401(k)&nbsp;of the Internal Revenue Code, for its U.S. employees.&#160; The plan allows eligible employees to defer, at the employee&#146;s discretion, pre-tax or post-tax compensation up to the IRS annual limits.&#160; Company contributions may be made at the discretion of the board of directors.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Effective as of January&nbsp;1, 2012, the Company amended its 401(k)&nbsp;plan to provide for employer matching contributions equal to (1)&nbsp;100% of employee deferral contributions up to a deferral rate of 3% of compensation, plus (2)&nbsp;50% of employee deferral contributions up to a deferral rate of an additional 2% of compensation.&#160; During the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 and 2013, the Company made aggregate matching contributions of $137,000 and $277,000, respectively.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">9. Commitments and Contingencies</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company leases approximately 23,800 square feet of office space in Waltham, Massachusetts under a non-cancelable operating lease agreement.&#160; The term of the lease commenced April&nbsp;1, 2013 and continues through March&nbsp;31, 2015.&#160; The lease agreement provides for one month of free rent with respect to a portion of the leased premises.&#160; The Company recognizes rental expense on a straight-line basis over the respective lease term including any free rent periods.&#160; The following table presents future minimum rental commitments, by fiscal year and in the aggregate, as of December&nbsp;31, 2013 (in thousands):</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In January&nbsp;2014, the Company entered into an amendment of its lease agreement whereby beginning in March&nbsp;2014, it expanded the total leased space in the facility to 53,200 square feet and extended the term of the lease through June&nbsp;30, 2017.&#160; The amended lease provides for additional rent expense of approximately $940,000 on an annualized basis.&#160; In addition, the amended lease increased the security deposit to approximately $0.7 million and continues to require the Company to pay a proportionate share of certain of the landlord&#146;s annual operating costs.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has entered into agreements with certain vendors for the provision of services, including services related to data management clinical operation support services, that the Company is not contractually able to terminate for convenience and avoid any and all future obligations to the vendors.&nbsp; Under such agreements, the Company is contractually obligated to make certain minimum payments to the vendors, with the exact amounts in the event of termination to be based on the timing of the termination and the exact terms of the agreement.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Litigation</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company may periodically become subject to legal proceedings and claims arising in connection with ongoing business activities, including claims or disputes related to patents that have been issued or that are pending in the field of research on which the Company is focused.&#160; The Company is not a party to any litigation and does not have contingency reserves established for any litigation liabilities.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">10. License Agreements</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Rolapitant In-License</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In December&nbsp;2010, the Company entered into a license agreement with OPKO Health,&nbsp;Inc., or OPKO, to obtain an exclusive, royalty-bearing, sublicensable worldwide license to research, develop, manufacture, market and sell rolapitant.&#160; The license agreement also extended to an additional, backup compound, SCH900978, to which the Company has the same rights and obligations as rolapitant, but which the Company is not currently advancing.&#160; Under the OPKO license the Company is obligated to use commercially reasonable efforts to conduct all preclinical, clinical, regulatory and other activities necessary to develop and commercialize rolapitant.&#160; Under the terms of the OPKO license, the Company paid OPKO $6.0&nbsp;million upon signing the agreement and issued 1,500,000 shares of its Junior Preferred Stock.&#160; At the time of the license transaction, the fair value of Junior Preferred Stock was determined to be $630,000.&#160; The Company is also required to make development milestone payments to OPKO of up to an aggregate of $30.0&nbsp;million if specified regulatory and initial commercial sales milestones are achieved.&#160; In addition, the Company is required to make additional milestone payments to OPKO of up to an aggregate of $85.0&nbsp;million if specified levels of annual net sales of rolapitant are achieved.&#160; If commercial sales of rolapitant commence, the Company is required to pay OPKO tiered royalties on the amount of annual net sales achieved in the United States and Europe at percentage rates that range from the low teens to the low twenties, which the Company expects will result in an effective royalty rate in the low teens.&#160; The royalty rate on annual net sales outside of the United States and Europe is slightly above the single digits.&#160; If the Company elects to develop and commercialize rolapitant in Japan through a third-party licensee the Company will share equally with OPKO all amounts received by it in connection with such activities under the Company&#146;s agreement with such third party, subject to certain exceptions and deductions.&#160; OPKO also retains an option to become the exclusive distributor of such products in Latin America, provided that OPKO exercises that option within a defined period following specified regulatory approvals in the United States.&#160; The Company is responsible for all preclinical, clinical, regulatory and other activities necessary to develop and commercialize rolapitant.&#160; There were no ongoing clinical trials for rolapitant at the time of its acquisition.&#160; As of the date of acquisition, none of the assets acquired had alternative future uses, nor had they reached a stage of technological feasibility.&#160; As no processes or activities that would constitute a &#147;business&#148; were acquired along with the license, the transaction was accounted for as an asset acquisition by recording the entire purchase price as acquired in-process research and development expense of $6.6&nbsp;million.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company has not made any additional milestone payments under this license agreement.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">TSR-011 In-License</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In March&nbsp;2011, the Company entered into a license agreement with Amgen,&nbsp;Inc., or Amgen, under which it received an exclusive, royalty bearing, sublicensable worldwide license under certain of Amgen&#146;s patent rights to research, develop, manufacture, market and sell licensed ALK inhibitor compounds, including TSR-011.&#160; The Company is also responsible for using commercially reasonable efforts to conduct all preclinical, clinical, regulatory and other activities necessary to develop</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">100<a name="PB_100_111655_9995"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>
<!-- SEQ.=1,FOLIO='100',FILE='C:\JMS\107901\13-25458-1\task6621304\25458-1-fp.htm',USER='107901',CD='Mar 13 11:17 2014' -->


<br clear="all" style="page-break-before:always;">
<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">and commercialize an ALK product.&#160; In the event that the Company wishes to sublicense any of the development and commercialization rights to any third party, it is required to grant to Amgen a right of first negotiation with respect to the rights it proposes to sublicense.&#160; Under the terms of the license agreement, in 2011 the Company made an up-front payment to Amgen of $0.5&nbsp;million.&#160; In November&nbsp;2012, in connection with the initiation of its Phase 1/2a clinical trial for TSR-011, the Company made a milestone payment to Amgen of $1.0 million.&#160; The Company is required to make total milestone payments to Amgen of up to an aggregate of $138.0&nbsp;million if specified clinical development, regulatory, initial commercialization and annual net product sales milestones are achieved.&#160; If commercial sales of a product commence, the Company will pay Amgen royalties at percentage rates ranging from the mid-single digits to slightly above the single digits based on cumulative worldwide net sales.&#160; At the time of the license transaction, TSR-011 was a preclinical compound.&#160; As of the date of acquisition, none of the assets acquired had alternative future uses, nor had they reached a stage of technological feasibility.&#160; As no processes or activities that would constitute a &#147;business&#148; were acquired along with the license, the transaction was accounted for as an asset acquisition and the entire purchase price of $0.5&nbsp;million was recorded as acquired in-process research and development expense.&#160; Milestone payments are also recorded as acquired in-process research and development and expensed as achieved.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Niraparib In-License</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In May&nbsp;2012, the Company entered into a license agreement with Merck Sharp&nbsp;&amp; Dohme Corp., a subsidiary of Merck&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;Co.,&nbsp;Inc., or Merck, under which the Company obtained exclusive, worldwide rights to certain patents and non-exclusive rights to certain Merck know-how, to research, develop, manufacture, market and sell niraparib and a backup compound, MK-2512, for all therapeutic and prophylactic uses in humans.&#160; The Company is not currently advancing MK-2512.&#160; Under the Merck license, the Company is obligated to use diligent efforts to develop and commercialize a licensed product.&#160; Under the terms of the license agreement, the Company was required to make an up-front payment to Merck of $7.0&nbsp;million in June&nbsp;2012.&#160; The Company is also required to make milestone payments to Merck of up to $57.0&nbsp;million in development and regulatory milestones for the first indication, up to $29.5&nbsp;million in development and regulatory milestones for each successive indication, and up to $87.5&nbsp;million in one-time sales milestones based on the achievement of annual sales objectives.&#160; If commercial sales of niraparib commence, the Company will pay Merck tiered royalties at a percentage rate in the low teens based on worldwide annual net sales.&#160; As of the date of acquisition, none of the assets acquired had alternative future uses, nor had they reached a stage of technological feasibility.&#160; As no processes or activities that would constitute a &#147;business&#148; were acquired along with the license, the transaction has been accounted for as an asset acquisition and the entire purchase price of $7.0&nbsp;million has been recorded as acquired in-process research and development expense.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the Company has made one milestone payment under this license agreement, in the amount of approximately $1.9 million, which became due </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">upon dosing of the first patient in the Phase 3 ovarian cancer clinical trial in July&nbsp;2013.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><i><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-style:italic;font-weight:bold;">Technology Licenses</font></i></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">In October&nbsp;2012, the Company entered into two license agreements with AstraZeneca UK Limited, having aggregate upfront payments of $0.4 million.&#160; These agreements provide the Company with the exclusive right to certain methods of treating patients with PARP inhibitors solely with respect to niraparib.&#160; Under certain circumstances, the Company may be required to make milestone and royalty payments to AstraZeneca UK Limited based on the achievement of certain development and regulatory milestone events with regard to niraparib, and on net sales of niraparib.&#160; The Company made milestone payments totaling $0.2 million to AstraZeneca during the year ended December&nbsp;31, 2013.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">11. Consolidated Quarterly Financial Data - Unaudited</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The following table presents unaudited consolidated quarterly financial data for the years ended December&nbsp;31, 2012 and 2013 (in thousands, except per share data):</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">101<a name="PB_101_111911_158"></a></font></p>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="1" width="25%" noshade color="black" align="left"></div>

<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;">(a)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="1" style="font-size:8.0pt;">In the quarter ended June&nbsp;30, 2012, the Company paid an up-front license fee to Merck for niraparib.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="1" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:8.0pt;">(b)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="1" style="font-size:8.0pt;">In July&nbsp;2012, the Company completed its initial public offering, which resulted in net proceeds of approximately $78.0 million from the issuance of 6,430,183 shares of common stock, which includes the sale of 430,183 shares under the underwriters&#146; over-allotment option.&#160; In connection with the initial public offering, all of the outstanding shares of the Company&#146;s convertible preferred stock were converted into 19,410,490 shares of common stock.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">12. Subsequent Event</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">On March 10, 2014, the Company entered into a c</font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">ollaboration and exclusive license agreement with AnaptysBio, Inc., or AnaptysBio, a privately-held therapeutic antibody company.&#160; Under the terms of this agreement, the Company obtained an exclusive, royalty-bearing, sublicenseable worldwide license to research, develop, manufacture, market and sell products based on AnaptysBio&#146;s </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;letter-spacing:-.15pt;">proprietary technology for the discovery, generation and optimization of certain specified immunotherapy antibodies.&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">AnaptysBio is responsible for performing initial discovery and development of therapeutic antibodies, with the goal of generating immunotherapy antibodies for use in the treatment of cancer.&#160; The Company is responsible for the performance and costs of all subsequent preclinical, clinical, regulatory, manufacturing and other activities necessary to develop and commercialize antibodies selected under each of three development programs and is obligated to use commercially reasonable efforts to research, develop or commercialize at least one product under each development program.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company is obligated to make an up-front, non-creditable and non-refundable cash payment of $17.0 million to AnaptysBio.&#160; The Company is also required to reimburse AnaptysBio, on a quarterly basis for up to two years from the effective date of the agreement, for specified costs incurred by AnaptysBio in its initial discovery and development activities covered by the agreement.&#160; Programs may be extended by mutual agreement of the parties and the Company can terminate on a program-by-program basis by providing 90 days prior written notice, subject to a wind-down period during which the Company&#146;s obligation to reimburse AnaptysBio for specified costs would continue.&#160; For each of the three development programs, the Company will be required to make milestone payments to AnaptysBio of up to $18.0 million if certain research and development milestone events are achieved, and up to an additional $90.0 million of milestone payments if certain U.S. and non-U.S. regulatory submissions and approvals occur in initial and subsequent indications.&#160; The Company will be required to pay AnaptysBio tiered single-digit royalties, on a product-by-product basis, on the amount of worldwide annual net sales achieved, and additional commercial milestone payments if specified levels of annual net sales of a product are attained.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The Company has </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">not yet completed its assessment of the accounting for this transaction.&#160; The Company expects to report its accounting for this transaction in its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ending March 31, 2014.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">102<a name="PB_102_111911_5753"></a></font></p>

<div style="font-family:Times New Roman;">
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;"><a href="#TableOfContents" title="Click to go to Table of Contents">Table of Contents</a></font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 9. CHANGES IN AND DISAGREEMENTS<a name="Item9_ChangesInAndDisagreements_165510"></a> WITH ACCOUNTANTS ON ACCOUNTING AND FINANCIAL DISCLOSURE</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">None.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 9A. CONTROLS AND PROCEDURES</font></b><a name="Item9a_ControlsAndProcedures_165514"></a></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Evaluation of Disclosure Controls and Procedures</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, management, with the participation of our Principal Executive Officer and Principal Financial Officer, performed an evaluation of the effectiveness of the design and operation of our disclosure controls and procedures as defined in Rules&nbsp;13a-15(e)&nbsp;and 15d-15(e)&nbsp;of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, or the Exchange Act.&#160; Our disclosure controls and procedures are designed to ensure that information required to be disclosed in the reports we file or submit under the Exchange Act is recorded, processed, summarized and reported within the time periods specified in the Securities and Exchange Commission&#146;s rules&nbsp;and forms, and that such information is accumulated and communicated to our management, including the Principal Executive Officer and the Principal Financial Officer, to allow timely decisions regarding required disclosures.&#160; Any controls and procedures, no matter how well designed and operated, can provide only reasonable assurance of achieving the desired control objective.&#160; Based on this evaluation, our Principal Executive Officer and Principal Financial Officer concluded that, as of December&nbsp;31, 2013, the design and operation of our disclosure controls and procedures were effective.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Management&#146;s Report on Internal Control Over Financial Reporting</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Our management is responsible for establishing and maintaining adequate internal control over financial reporting.&#160; Internal control over financial reporting is defined in Rule&nbsp;13a-15(f)&nbsp;or 15d-15(f)&nbsp;under the Exchange Act as a process designed by, or under the supervision of, our principal executive officer and principal financial officer and effected by our board of directors, management, and other personnel, to provide reasonable assurance regarding the reliability of financial reporting and the preparation of our financial statements for external reporting purposes in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles and includes those policies and procedures that:</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">pertain to the maintenance of records that in reasonable detail accurately and fairly reflect the transactions and dispositions of our assets;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">provide reasonable assurance that transactions are recorded as necessary to permit preparation of financial statements in conformity with generally accepted accounting principles, and that our receipts and expenditures are being made only in accordance with authorizations of our management and directors; and</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Symbol" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&#183;</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">provide reasonable assurance regarding prevention or timely detection of unauthorized acquisition, use or disposition of our assets that could have a material effect on the consolidated financial statements.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Because of its inherent limitations, internal control over financial reporting may not prevent or detect misstatements.&#160; Projections of any evaluation of effectiveness to future periods are subject to the risk that controls may become inadequate because of changes in conditions, or that the degree of compliance with the policies or procedures may deteriorate.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">Under the supervision of and with the participation of our principal executive officer and principal financial officer, our management assessed the effectiveness of our internal control over financial reporting as of December&nbsp;31, 2013 based on the criteria set forth by the Committee of Sponsoring Organizations of the Treadway Commission (COSO) in 1992 in&nbsp;<i>Internal Control&#151;Integrated Framework</i>.&#160; Based on this assessment, management concluded that our internal control over financial reporting was effective as of December&nbsp;31, 2013.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">Changes in Internal Control Over Financial Reporting</font></b></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">During the quarter ended December&nbsp;31, 2013, there were no changes in our internal control over financial reporting that materially affected, or are reasonably likely to materially affect, our internal control over financial reporting.</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p align="center" style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-align:center;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">103<a name="PB_103_111911_1002"></a></font></p>
<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="3" width="100%" noshade color="#010101" align="left"></div>
</div>

<div style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><hr size="1" width="25%" noshade color="black" align="left"></div>
</div>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(1)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, 165,830 shares were authorized for issuance under our 2012 Omnibus Incentive Plan, or the 2012 Incentive Plan, which became effective in April&nbsp;2012, including 6,857 remaining shares that were then available for future issuance under the 2010 Stock Incentive Plan, or the 2010 Incentive Plan, which were transferred to the 2012 Incentive Plan.&#160; The number of shares of our common stock reserved for issuance under the 2012 Incentive Plan will be increased (i)&nbsp;from time to time by the number of shares of our common stock forfeited upon the expiration, cancellation, forfeiture, cash settlement or other termination of awards under the 2010 Incentive Plan and (ii)&nbsp;on January&nbsp;1 of each year, starting in 2014, by a number of shares of common stock equal to the lesser of (x)&nbsp;4% of the shares of common stock outstanding at such time or (y)&nbsp;the number of shares determined by our board of directors.&#160; As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, 17,262 shares of our common stock had been cancelled under the 2010 Incentive Plan and transferred to the 2012 Incentive Plan.&#160; Effective January&nbsp;1, 2014, the number of shares authorized for issuance under the 2012 Incentive Plan was increased by 1,309,560 shares.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">(2)</font><font size="1" style="font-size:3.0pt;">&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160;&#160; </font><font size="2" style="font-size:10.0pt;">As of December&nbsp;31, 2013, 267,169 shares were reserved for issuance under our 2012 Employee Stock Purchase Plan, or ESPP, which became effective in June&nbsp;2012.</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt .75in;text-indent:-.25in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><b><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;font-weight:bold;">ITEM 13. CERTAIN RELATIONSHIPS AND RELATED TRANSACTIONS, AND DIRECTOR INDEPENDENCE</font></b><a name="Item13_CertainRelationshipsAndRel_170135"></a></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">&nbsp;</font></p>
<p style="margin:0in 0in .0001pt;text-indent:.5in;"><font size="2" face="Times New Roman" style="font-size:10.0pt;">The information required by this Item is set forth in our 2014 Proxy Statement to be filed with the SEC within 120 days of December&nbsp;31, 2013, and is incorporated into this Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K by reference.</font></p>
